steps.title: Complete the sentence directly
steps.content: The sentence is saying what the average summer temperature in Tallinn is: "the average summer temperature ______ just 66.7 degrees Fahrenheit." The subject is "the average summer temperature," and "just 66.7 degrees Fahrenheit" is the complement that completes the idea.

steps.title: Avoid interrupting the verb
steps.content: A linking verb should connect the subject directly to its complement. No punctuation is needed between the verb and the number because the words after the blank are not a separate sentence or an explanation introduced by punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate punctuation choices
steps.content: Choice B, "is;", uses a semicolon before words that cannot stand as an independent clause. Choice C, "is:", uses a colon after an incomplete thought. Choice D, "is," places a comma between the linking verb and its complement. The plain verb completes the sentence correctly. The answer is A.
